v.CNV: 我的妹妹，我的新妇啊！我进了我的园中；我采了我的没药与香料；我吃了我的蜂房与蜂蜜；我喝了我的酒和奶。朋友们！你们要开怀吃喝；亲爱的啊！你们要不醉无归。
v.ESV: I came to my garden, my sister, my bride, I gathered my myrrh with my spice, I ate my honeycomb with my honey, I drank my wine with my milk. Eat, friends, drink, and be drunk with love!
v.GAE: 내 누이, 내 신부야 내가 내 동산에 들어와서 나의 몰약과 향 재료를 거두고 나의 꿀송이와 꿀을 먹고 내 포도주와 내 우유를 마셨으니 나의 친구들아 먹으라 나의 사랑하는 사람들아 많이 마시라
v.KJV: I am come into my garden, my sister, my spouse: I have gathered my myrrh with my spice; I have eaten my honeycomb with my honey; I have drunk my wine with my milk: eat, O friends; drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved.
v.NASB: 'I have come into my garden, my sister, my bride; I have gathered my myrrh along with my balsam. I have eaten my honeycomb and my honey; I have drunk my wine and my milk. Eat, friends; Drink and imbibe deeply, O lovers.'
v.NIV: I have come into my garden, my sister, my bride; I have gathered my myrrh with my spice. I have eaten my honeycomb and my honey; I have drunk my wine and my milk. Eat, friends, and drink; drink your fill of love.
v.NRSV: I come to my garden, my sister, my bride; I gather my myrrh with my spice, I eat my honeycomb with my honey, I drink my wine with my milk. Eat, friends, drink, and be drunk with love.
v.SAENEW: 나의 누이, 나의 신부야! 나의 동산으로 내가 찾아왔다. 몰약과 향료를 거두고, 꿀과 꿀송이를 따먹고, 포도주와 젖도 마셨다. (친구들) 먹어라, 마셔라, 친구들아! 사랑에 흠뻑 취하여라. (여자)
v.WLB: 내 누이여, 내 신부여, 내가 내 정원을 찾아왔습니다. 내 향품과 함께 몰약을 거두고 내 꿀송이와 꿀을 먹고 내 포도주와 젖을 마셨습니다. 친구들이여, 먹고 마셔요. 사랑하는 사람들이여, 마음껏 들어요.

v.CNV: 我身虽然睡卧，我心却醒。这是我的良人的声音，他敲着门说：“我的妹妹，我的佳偶，我的鸽子，我的完全人哪！求你给我开门；因为我的头满了露水，头发给夜露滴湿。”
v.ESV: I slept, but my heart was awake. A sound! My beloved is knocking. “Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my perfect one, for my head is wet with dew, my locks with the drops of the night.”
v.GAE: 내가 잘지라도 마음은 깨었는데 나의 사랑하는 자의 소리가 들리는구나 문을 두드려 이르기를 나의 누이, 나의 사랑, 나의 비둘기, 나의 완전한 자야 문을 열어 다오 내 머리에는 이슬이, 내 머리털에는 밤이슬이 가득하였다 하는구나
v.KJV: I sleep, but my heart waketh: it is the voice of my beloved that knocketh, saying, Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my undefiled: for my head is filled with dew, and my locks with the drops of the night.
v.NASB: 'I was asleep but my heart was awake. A voice! My beloved was knocking: 'Open to me, my sister, my darling, My dove, my perfect one! For my head is drenched with dew, My locks with the damp of the night.'
v.NIV: I slept but my heart was awake. Listen! My beloved is knocking: “Open to me, my sister, my darling, my dove, my flawless one. My head is drenched with dew, my hair with the dampness of the night.”
v.NRSV: I slept, but my heart was awake. Listen! my beloved is knocking. “Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my perfect one; for my head is wet with dew, my locks with the drops of the night.”
v.SAENEW: 나는 자고 있었지만, 나의 마음은 깨어 있었다. 저 소리, 나의 사랑하는 이가 문을 두드리는 소리. "문 열어요! 나의 누이, 나의 사랑, 티없이 맑은 나의 비둘기! 머리가 온통 이슬에 젖고, 머리채가 밤이슬에 흠뻑 젖었소."
v.WLB: 나는 자고 있었지만 내 마음은 깨어 있었습니다. 들어 보세요! 내가 사랑하는 그분이 문을 두드리시네요. 내 누이여, 내 사랑이여, 문을 열어 주세요. 내 비둘기여, 흠 없는 내 사람이여, 내 머리는 이슬에 흠뻑 젖었고 내 머리칼에는 밤이슬이 가득합니다.

v.CNV: 我回答：“我脱了外衣，怎能再穿上呢？我洗了脚，怎能再弄脏呢？”
v.ESV: I had put off my garment; how could I put it on? I had bathed my feet; how could I soil them?
v.GAE: 내가 옷을 벗었으니 어찌 다시 입겠으며 내가 발을 씻었으니 어찌 다시 더럽히랴마는
v.KJV: I have put off my coat; how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?
v.NASB: 'I have taken off my dress, How can I put it on again? I have washed my feet, How can I dirty them again?
v.NIV: I have taken off my robe— must I put it on again? I have washed my feet— must I soil them again?
v.NRSV: I had put off my garment; how could I put it on again? I had bathed my feet; how could I soil them?
v.SAENEW: 아, 나는 벌써 옷을 벗었는데, 다시 입어야 하나? 발도 씻었는데, 다시 흙을 묻혀야 하나?
v.WLB: 나는 이미 옷을 벗었는데 다시 입어야 하나요? 나는 발을 씻었는데 다시 더럽혀야 하나요?

v.CNV: 我的良人从门孔里伸进手来，我的心因他大为激动。
v.ESV: My beloved put his hand to the latch, and my heart was thrilled within me.
v.GAE: 내 사랑하는 자가 문틈으로 손을 들이밀매 내 마음이 움직여서
v.KJV: My beloved put in his hand by the hole of the door, and my bowels were moved for him.
v.NASB: 'My beloved extended his hand through the opening, And my feelings were aroused for him.
v.NIV: My beloved thrust his hand through the latch-opening; my heart began to pound for him.
v.NRSV: My beloved thrust his hand into the opening, and my inmost being yearned for him.
v.SAENEW: 사랑하는 이가 문 틈으로 손을 들이밀 때에, 아, 설레이는 나의 마음.
v.WLB: 내가 사랑하는 그분이 문틈으로 손을 들이미시니 내 마음이 그분으로 인해 두근거리기 시작합니다.

v.CNV: 我起来，要给我的良人开门；我的两手滴下没药，指头滴下没药，滴在门闩上。
v.ESV: I arose to open to my beloved, and my hands dripped with myrrh, my fingers with liquid myrrh, on the handles of the bolt.
v.GAE: 일어나 내 사랑하는 자를 위하여 문을 열 때 몰약이 내 손에서, 몰약의 즙이 내 손가락에서 문빗장에 떨어지는구나
v.KJV: I rose up to open to my beloved; and my hands dropped with myrrh, and my fingers with sweet smelling myrrh, upon the handles of the lock.
v.NASB: 'I arose to open to my beloved; And my hands dripped with myrrh, And my fingers with liquid myrrh, On the handles of the bolt.
v.NIV: I arose to open for my beloved, and my hands dripped with myrrh, my fingers with flowing myrrh, on the handles of the bolt.
v.NRSV: I arose to open to my beloved, and my hands dripped with myrrh, my fingers with liquid myrrh, upon the handles of the bolt.
v.SAENEW: 사랑하는 이를 맞아들이려고 벌떡 일어나서 몰약에 젖은 손으로, 몰약의 즙이 뚝뚝 듣는 손가락으로 문빗장을 잡았지.
v.WLB: 내가 사랑하는 그분을 위해 문을 열려고 일어날 때 몰약이 내 손에서, 몰약의 즙이 내 손가락을 타고 흘러 문빗장에 닿습니다.

v.CNV: 我亲手给我的良人开门，我的良人却已转身走了；我发现他走了，差点昏倒；我到处找他，却找不见，我呼叫他，他却不回答。
v.ESV: I opened to my beloved, but my beloved had turned and gone. My soul failed me when he spoke. I sought him, but found him not; I called him, but he gave no answer.
v.GAE: 내가 내 사랑하는 자를 위하여 문을 열었으나 그는 벌써 물러갔네 그가 말할 때에 내 혼이 나갔구나 내가 그를 찾아도 못 만났고 불러도 응답이 없었노라
v.KJV: I opened to my beloved; but my beloved had withdrawn himself, and was gone: my soul failed when he spake: I sought him, but I could not find him; I called him, but he gave me no answer.
v.NASB: 'I opened to my beloved, But my beloved had turned away and had gone! My heart went out to him as he spoke. I searched for him but I did not find him; I called him but he did not answer me.
v.NIV: I opened for my beloved, but my beloved had left; he was gone. My heart sank at his departure. I looked for him but did not find him. I called him but he did not answer.
v.NRSV: I opened to my beloved, but my beloved had turned and was gone. My soul failed me when he spoke. I sought him, but did not find him; I called him, but he gave no answer.
v.SAENEW: 사랑하는 이를 맞아들이려고 문을 열었지. 그러나 나의 임은 몸을 돌려 가 버리네. 임의 말에 넋을 잃고 그를 찾아 나섰으나, 가버린 그를 찾을 수 없네. 불러도 대답이 없네.
v.WLB: 내가 사랑하는 그분을 위해 문을 열었는데 내 사랑하는 그분은 떠나고 없네요. 그분이 없어 내 마음은 무너져 내립니다. 그분을 찾았지만 찾을 수 없습니다. 그분을 불렀지만 대답이 없습니다.

v.CNV: 城中的守卫巡逻的时候，找着我；他们打了我，伤了我；看守城墙的人夺去了我身上的外衣。
v.ESV: The watchmen found me as they went about in the city; they beat me, they bruised me, they took away my veil, those watchmen of the walls.
v.GAE: 성 안을 순찰하는 자들이 나를 만나매 나를 쳐서 상하게 하였고 성벽을 파수하는 자들이 나의 겉옷을 벗겨 가졌도다
v.KJV: The watchmen that went about the city found me, they smote me, they wounded me; the keepers of the walls took away my veil from me.
v.NASB: 'The watchmen who make the rounds in the city found me, They struck me and wounded me; The guardsmen of the walls took away my shawl from me.
v.NIV: The watchmen found me as they made their rounds in the city. They beat me, they bruised me; they took away my cloak, those watchmen of the walls!
v.NRSV: Making their rounds in the city the sentinels found me; they beat me, they wounded me, they took away my mantle, those sentinels of the walls.
v.SAENEW: 성읍을 순찰하는 야경꾼들이 나를 때려서 상처를 입히고, 성벽을 지키는 파수꾼들이 나의 겉옷을 벗기네.
v.WLB: 성안을 순찰하던 파수꾼들이 나를 발견하고는 나를 때려 상처를 내고 저 성벽을 지키는 사람들은 내 겉옷을 빼앗아 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 耶路撒冷的众女子啊！我嘱咐你们：你们若遇见我的良人，你们要告诉他什么呢？你们要告诉他，我患了相思病。
v.ESV: I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if you find my beloved, that you tell him I am sick with love.
v.GAE: 예루살렘 딸들아 너희에게 내가 부탁한다 너희가 내 사랑하는 자를 만나거든 내가 사랑하므로 병이 났다고 하려무나
v.KJV: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if ye find my beloved, that ye tell him, that I am sick of love.
v.NASB: 'I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, If you find my beloved, As to what you will tell him: For I am lovesick.'
v.NIV: Daughters of Jerusalem, I charge you — if you find my beloved, what will you tell him? Tell him I am faint with love.
v.NRSV: I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if you find my beloved, tell him this: I am faint with love.
v.SAENEW: 부탁하자, 예루살렘의 아가씨들아, 너희가 나의 임을 만나거든, 내가 사랑 때문에 병들었다고 말하여 다오. (친구들)
v.WLB: 예루살렘의 딸들이여, 내가 부탁합니다. 만약 내가 사랑하는 그분을 만나거든 내가 사랑 때문에 병이 났다고 말해 주세요.

v.CNV: 你这女子中最美丽的啊！你的良人比别人的良人有什么更可爱之处呢？你的良人比别人的良人有什么更可爱之处呢？以致你这样嘱咐我们呢？
v.ESV: What is your beloved more than another beloved, O most beautiful among women? What is your beloved more than another beloved, that you thus adjure us?
v.GAE: 여자들 가운데에 어여쁜 자야 너의 사랑하는 자가 남의 사랑하는 자보다 나은 것이 무엇인가 너의 사랑하는 자가 남의 사랑하는 자보다 나은 것이 무엇이기에 이같이 우리에게 부탁하는가
v.KJV: What is thy beloved more than another beloved, O thou fairest among women? what is thy beloved more than another beloved, that thou dost so charge us?
v.NASB: 'What kind of beloved is your beloved, O most beautiful among women? What kind of beloved is your beloved, That thus you adjure us?'
v.NIV: How is your beloved better than others, most beautiful of women? How is your beloved better than others, that you so charge us?
v.NRSV: What is your beloved more than another beloved, O fairest among women? What is your beloved more than another beloved, that you thus adjure us?
v.SAENEW: 여인들 가운데서도 빼어나게 예쁜 여인아, 너의 임이 다른 임보다 무엇이 더 나으냐? 너의 임이 어떤 임이기에, 네가 우리에게 그런 부탁을 하느냐? (여자)
v.WLB: 가장 아름다운 여자여, 당신이 사랑하는 분이 다른 이보다 얼마나 더 나은가요? 당신이 사랑하는 분이 다른 이보다 얼마나 낫기에 우리에게 이렇게 부탁하나요?

v.CNV: 我的良人红光满面，是万人中的表表者。
v.ESV: My beloved is radiant and ruddy, distinguished among ten thousand.
v.GAE: 내 사랑하는 자는 희고도 붉어 많은 사람 가운데에 뛰어나구나
v.KJV: My beloved is white and ruddy, the chiefest among ten thousand.
v.NASB: 'My beloved is dazzling and ruddy, Outstanding among ten thousand.
v.NIV: My beloved is radiant and ruddy, outstanding among ten thousand.
v.NRSV: My beloved is all radiant and ruddy, distinguished among ten thousand.
v.SAENEW: 나의 임은 깨끗한 살결에 혈색 좋은 미남이다. 만인 가운데 으뜸이다.
v.WLB: 내가 사랑하는 그분은 깨끗한 외모에 혈색이 붉은 건강한 분이랍니다. 수많은 사람들 가운데서도 뛰어나지요.

v.CNV: 他的头像精金，他的头发如棕树枝厚密，像乌鸦那么黑。
v.ESV: His head is the finest gold; his locks are wavy, black as a raven.
v.GAE: 머리는 순금 같고 머리털은 고불고불하고 까마귀 같이 검구나
v.KJV: His head is as the most fine gold, his locks are bushy, and black as a raven.
v.NASB: 'His head is like gold, pure gold; His locks are like clusters of dates And black as a raven.
v.NIV: His head is purest gold; his hair is wavy and black as a raven.
v.NRSV: His head is the finest gold; his locks are wavy, black as a raven.
v.SAENEW: 머리는 정금이고, 곱슬거리는 머리채는 까마귀같이 검다.
v.WLB: 그의 머리는 순금 같고 굽이치는 머릿결은 까마귀처럼 검습니다.

v.CNV: 他的眼好像溪水旁的鸽子，在奶中洗净，塑得合式。
v.ESV: His eyes are like doves beside streams of water, bathed in milk, sitting beside a full pool.
v.GAE: 눈은 시냇가의 비둘기 같은데 우유로 씻은 듯하고 아름답게도 박혔구나
v.KJV: His eyes are as the eyes of doves by the rivers of waters, washed with milk, and fitly set.
v.NASB: 'His eyes are like doves Beside streams of water, Bathed in milk, And reposed in their setting.
v.NIV: His eyes are like doves by the water streams, washed in milk, mounted like jewels.
v.NRSV: His eyes are like doves beside springs of water, bathed in milk, fitly set.
v.SAENEW: 그의 두 눈은 흐르는 물 가에 앉은 비둘기. 젖으로 씻은 듯, 넘실거리는 못 가에 앉은 모습이다.
v.WLB: 그의 눈은 시냇가의 비둘기 같은데 젖으로 목욕한 듯 보석처럼 아름답게 박혀 있습니다.

v.CNV: 他的两颊如香花畦，又如香草台；他的嘴唇像百合花，滴下没药汁。
v.ESV: His cheeks are like beds of spices, mounds of sweet-smelling herbs. His lips are lilies, dripping liquid myrrh.
v.GAE: 뺨은 향기로운 꽃밭 같고 향기로운 풀언덕과도 같고 입술은 백합화 같고 몰약의 즙이 뚝뚝 떨어지는구나
v.KJV: His cheeks are as a bed of spices, as sweet flowers: his lips like lilies, dropping sweet smelling myrrh.
v.NASB: 'His cheeks are like a bed of balsam, Banks of sweet-scented herbs; His lips are lilies Dripping with liquid myrrh.
v.NIV: His cheeks are like beds of spice yielding perfume. His lips are like lilies dripping with myrrh.
v.NRSV: His cheeks are like beds of spices, yielding fragrance. His lips are lilies, distilling liquid myrrh.
v.SAENEW: 그의 두 볼은 향기 가득한 꽃밭, 향내음 풍기는 풀언덕이요, 그의 입술은 몰약의 즙이 뚝뚝 듣는 나리꽃이다.
v.WLB: 그의 볼은 향기로 가득한 꽃밭 같고 그의 입술은 몰약이 뚝뚝 떨어지는 백합화 같습니다.

v.CNV: 他的双手像金管，镶嵌着水苍玉；他的躯体如象牙块，周围包着蓝宝石。
v.ESV: His arms are rods of gold, set with jewels. His body is polished ivory, bedecked with sapphires.
v.GAE: 손은 황옥을 물린 황금 노리개 같고 몸은 아로새긴 상아에 청옥을 입힌 듯하구나
v.KJV: His hands are as gold rings set with the beryl: his belly is as bright ivory overlaid with sapphires.
v.NASB: 'His hands are rods of gold Set with beryl; His abdomen is carved ivory Inlaid with sapphires.
v.NIV: His arms are rods of gold set with topaz. His body is like polished ivory decorated with lapis lazuli.
v.NRSV: His arms are rounded gold, set with jewels. His body is ivory work, encrusted with sapphires.
v.SAENEW: 그의 손은 가지런하고, 보석 박은 반지를 끼었다. 그의 허리는 청옥 입힌 상아처럼 미끈하다.
v.WLB: 그의 두 팔은 황옥이 박힌 황금 방망이 같고 그의 몸은 사파이어로 장식한 매끄러운 상아 같습니다.

v.CNV: 他的双腿像大理石柱，安放在精金的座上；他的容貌如黎巴嫩山，像佳美的香柏树。
v.ESV: His legs are alabaster columns, set on bases of gold. His appearance is like Lebanon, choice as the cedars.
v.GAE: 다리는 순금 받침에 세운 화반석 기둥 같고 생김새는 레바논 같으며 백향목처럼 보기 좋고
v.KJV: His legs are as pillars of marble, set upon sockets of fine gold: his countenance is as Lebanon, excellent as the cedars.
v.NASB: 'His legs are pillars of alabaster Set on pedestals of pure gold; His appearance is like Lebanon Choice as the cedars.
v.NIV: His legs are pillars of marble set on bases of pure gold. His appearance is like Lebanon, choice as its cedars.
v.NRSV: His legs are alabaster columns, set upon bases of gold. His appearance is like Lebanon, choice as the cedars.
v.SAENEW: 그의 두 다리는 순금 받침대 위에 선 대리석 기둥이다. 그는 레바논처럼 늠름하고, 백향목처럼 훤칠하다.
v.WLB: 그의 두 다리는 순금 받침 위에 세운 대리석 기둥 같고 그의 모습은 레바논 같고 백향목처럼 뛰어납니다.

v.CNV: 他的口甜蜜；他全然可爱。耶路撒冷的众女子啊！这就是我的良人，我的朋友。
v.ESV: His mouth is most sweet, and he is altogether desirable. This is my beloved and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.
v.GAE: 입은 심히 달콤하니 그 전체가 사랑스럽구나 예루살렘 딸들아 이는 내 사랑하는 자요 나의 친구로다
v.KJV: His mouth is most sweet: yea, he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved, and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.
v.NASB: 'His mouth is full of sweetness. And he is wholly desirable. This is my beloved and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.'
v.NIV: His mouth is sweetness itself; he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved, this is my friend, daughters of Jerusalem.
v.NRSV: His speech is most sweet, and he is altogether desirable. This is my beloved and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.
v.SAENEW: 그의 입 속은 달콤하고, 그에게 있는 것은 모두 사랑스럽다. 예루살렘의 아가씨들아, 이 사람이 바로 나의 임, 나의 친구이다.
v.WLB: 그의 입은 달콤하기 그지없으니 그가 가진 모든 것이 사랑스럽습니다. 예루살렘의 딸들이여, 이분이 바로 내가 사랑하는 그분, 내 친구입니다.
